# PEDS Exam 2 Cheatsheet — Ch20-24

**Topics:** Disorders & Diseases of Infants, Toddlers, and School-Age

## EYE/VISION DISORDERS
- Strabismus: eye misalignment; correct with lenses/patching
- Amblyopia: "lazy eye"; patch GOOD eye to strengthen weak one
- Screening: cover test, corneal light reflex

## NEUROLOGICAL
- Meningitis: fever, nuchal rigidity, Kernig/Brudzinski, photophobia; CSF cloudy (bacterial); droplet precautions; antibiotics after cultures
- Seizures: clear area, protect head, side-lying, time seizure; NEVER restrain or put anything in mouth; rescue med (diazepam) if ordered
- Hydroceph: bulging fontanel, increased HC, sunset eyes, high-pitched cry; VP shunt malfunction: vomiting, headache, altered LOC
- CP types: spastic (most common), dyskinetic, ataxic; PT/OT to prevent contractures

## MUSCULOSKELETAL
- Scoliosis: Adams forward bend test (rib hump); <20° monitor, 20-40° bracing, >40° surgery; logroll post-op
- DDH: Ortolani (abduct→click), Barlow (adduct→click); asymmetric thigh folds; Pavlik harness 6-12 weeks
- Clubfoot: Ponseti method (serial casting); Achilles tenotomy may be needed
- Fractures: 5 P's (Pain, Pallor, Paresthesia, Pulselessness, Paralysis); cast care: fiberglass can get wet, plaster keep dry
- Traction: Bryant (infants), Buck/Russell (older); weights hang free; neuro checks; skin integrity
- Duchenne MD: X-linked recessive (boys); Gower's sign, pseudohypertrophy calves; progressive → wheelchair
- Legg-Calvé-Perthes: avascular necrosis femoral head (school-age); limp, hip pain; rest/bracing
- SCFE: obese adolescents; pain, limp, limited internal rotation; surgical pinning
- JIA: joint inflammation >6 weeks; NSAIDs, DMARDs, PT; eye exams (uveitis)

## GI/ABDOMINAL
- Pyloric stenosis: projectile vomiting, olive mass, hunger after; surgical emergency
- Intussusception: "currant jelly" stools, sausage mass, pain; barium enema or surgery
- Hirschsprung: failure to pass meconium, ribbon stools, abdominal distention
- Celiac: gluten intolerance; lifelong gluten-free diet; failure to thrive
- Dehydration: sunken fontanel, dry MM, decreased UOP, delayed cap refill; ORS for mild, IV for mod/severe

## ENT/RESPIRATORY
- Otitis media: ear pain, fever, tugging; pull pinna DOWN/BACK (infants), UP/BACK (older); smoke exposure risk
- Croup: barky cough, stridor; cool mist, racemic epi, steroids; keep calm
- Epiglottitis: drooling, tripod, high fever, muffled voice; EMERGENCY — do NOT examine throat; prepare airway
- Bronchiolitis (RSV): infants 2-12mo; wheezing, apnea; supportive care, suction, hydration
- Asthma: albuterol rescue; peak flow; avoid triggers; status asthmaticus = emergency
- Tonsillectomy: frequent swallowing = hemorrhage; cool liquids, no red liquids, no straws

## INFECTIOUS/COMMUNICABLE
- Reye syndrome: aspirin + viral illness → encephalopathy + fatty liver; avoid aspirin in children
- CF: dornase alfa, CPT, high-calorie diet, enzymes with meals; hand hygiene before eating
- Varicella: vesicular rash; contagious until crusted; vaccine prevents
- Fifth disease: "slapped cheek" → lacy rash; mild in children, dangerous in pregnancy
- Roseola: high fever → rash after fever drops
- Scarlet fever: sandpaper rash, strawberry tongue; post-strep
- Impetigo: contagious skin infection; hand hygiene, no sharing towels

## CARDIAC
- Tetralogy of Fallot: VSD+PS+overriding aorta+RVH; cyanosis, "tet" spells (knee-chest position, O2, morphine)
- PDA: increased pulmonary blood flow; murmur
- Coarctation: decreased BP/pulses in legs
- Kawasaki: fever >5 days, strawberry tongue, rash, conjunctivitis; IVIG + aspirin; coronary aneurysm risk

## HEMATOLOGY/ONCOLOGY
- Leukemia: fever, bruising, bone pain, pallor, ANC<500 = neutropenia; neutropenic fever = emergency (antibiotics immediately)
- Sickle cell: hydration, pain mgmt, avoid ice, avoid cold; crisis = IV fluids, O2, analgesics
- Hemophilia: factor replacement, ice/elevation, avoid NSAIDs/aspirin
- Iron-deficiency anemia: give with vit C, empty stomach; avoid milk with iron

## ENDOCRINE
- Type 1 diabetes: polyuria/polydipsia/polyphagia; sick days: check glucose frequently, NEVER skip insulin; DKA = fruity breath, vomiting, IV fluids + insulin
- Hypoglycemia: 15g fast-acting carb, recheck 15min

## DEVELOPMENT/SAFETY
- Erikson: Trust (infant), Autonomy (toddler), Initiative (preschool), Industry (school-age)
- Injury prevention: choking, falls, poisoning, drowning, MVC, sports
- Abuse/neglect: spiral fractures, inconsistent stories, glove/stocking burns; mandated reporter
- Failure to thrive: poor weight gain, developmental delay; organic vs nonorganic

## NURSING PRIORITIES
- ABCs always first
- Neurovascular checks (5 P's) for fractures/traction
- Meningitis: droplet precautions, ICP monitoring, antibiotics after cultures
- Seizures: side-lying, protect head, time, NO restraint
- Epiglottitis: airway emergency, don't examine throat
- Post-op hemorrhage: frequent swallowing (tonsil), bright red vomitus
- ICP infant: bulging fontanel, high-pitched cry, setting-sun eyes
- Dehydration: weight trends, cap refill, MM, UOP

## HIGH-YIELD
- Adams test: scoliosis
- Ortolani/Barlow: DDH
- Gower's sign: Duchenne
- 5 P's: neurovascular
- Kernig/Brudzinski: meningitis
- Sunset eyes: hydroceph
- Currant jelly stools: intussusception
- Olive mass: pyloric stenosis
- Drooling + tripod: epiglottitis
- Barky cough: croup
- Aspirin + viral → Reye
- Spiral fracture → abuse
- Neutropenic fever → antibiotics immediately
- Post-tonsil frequent swallowing → hemorrhage

*All tested content verified*
